steps.title: Find the slope from the two points
steps.content:
Use the slope formula with points $(2, 3)$ and $(6, 5)$:
$$\frac{5 - 3}{6 - 2} = \frac{2}{4} = \frac{1}{2}$$

steps.title: Find the y-intercept and write the equation
steps.content:
Substitute point $(2, 3)$ and slope $m = \frac{1}{2}$:
$$3 = \frac{1}{2}(2) + b$$
$$3 = 1 + b$$
$$b = 2$$
So the equation is $f(x) = \frac{1}{2}x + 2$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
